description: Define the LibraryEntry and LibraryRegistry TypeScript interfaces, create the initial library-registry.json with the hx-library default entry, and implement all file I/O utilities (read, write, add, update, remove, validate). This is the critical-path foundation — API routes and UI both depend on these types and store functions. Includes: (1) apps/admin/src/data/library-registry.json with one default entry for hx-library (source: 'local', cemPath pointing to the existing CEM path, prefix: 'hx-', id: 'hx-library'). (2) apps/admin/src/lib/registry/types.ts defining LibraryEntry { id, name, source, cemPath?, packageName?, prefix, lastScored?, componentCount?, mcpConfig? } and LibraryRegistry { version, entries: LibraryEntry[] }. (3) apps/admin/src/lib/registry/registry-store.ts exporting: readRegistry(): Promise<LibraryRegistry>, writeRegistry(r): Promise<void>, addEntry(e): Promise<LibraryEntry>, updateEntry(id, patch): Promise<LibraryEntry>, removeEntry(id): Promise<void>, validateEntry(e): { valid: boolean, errors: string[] }. Validation rules: id must be slug-safe, source must be 'local'|'cdn'|'npm', local entries require cemPath, cdn/npm entries require packageName, prefix must end with '-'. Use Node fs/promises for file I/O; path must resolve relative to process.cwd() so it works in both dev and prod Next.js.
title: Phase 1: Registry Data Model, JSON File & CRUD Utilities
